override_reason: Lovejoy anchor (C/2011 W3) adopted over JWST 0.38 km nucleus measurement. Additionally, assumed Pe-subgroup (Pop I) analogy but inbound orbital period ~1,695 yr is anomalous for that cluster — consistent with a novel second-generation subgroup (possible 371 BC / AD 363 lineage) with zero documented modern survivors at this size scale.
anchor_mismatch_reason: Lovejoy survived perihelion. MAPS did not. Using a survivor to calibrate disintegration probability is survivorship bias in reference class selection — not merely a size-scaling error.
correct_anchor: C/2024 S1 (Atlas) — disintegrated pre-perihelion, thermal cascade / rotational spin-up, nucleus ~0.25–0.35 km, no post-perihelion nucleus recovered
subgroup_epistemic_qualifier: MAPS orbital subgroup uncertain. Inbound period ~1,695 yr is anomalous for Pe-subgroup. If novel subgroup, no documented modern survivor exists at ≥0.38 km in that class. Wolfram PDis formula gives necessary but not sufficient bound. Novel subgroup uncertainty adds ±0.25 to disruption probability band.

three_path_convergence.convergence: All three paths agree within stated uncertainty — robust.

outcome: CONFIRMED (binary: disintegration occurred, P1=0.55 >= 50%) / MISCALIBRATED PROBABILITY (Brier penalty 0.18)
lesson: Anchor selection must match survival-outcome class — never use a survivor to calibrate a disintegrator
Subgroup orbital classification must be verified pre-lock via full-period backward integration
JWST nucleus measurement was accurate — should have anchored on it directly
Novel-subgroup status triggers mandatory widening of uncertainty band by ±0.25
Atlas was the correct analog — identified post-perihelion, not pre-lock
revision_rationale: v3 external audit added survivorship bias finding and subgroup epistemic qualifier. Score unchanged. Wolfram UUID added from B2 UUID pass (April 10, 2026).
